description_long: This structure is a rectangular temple with a prominent colonnade of evenly spaced columns along its longer sides, reminiscent of ancient Greco-Roman architecture. The front features a wide staircase leading up to a portico with a triangular pediment supported by columns, creating a grand entrance. The rear of the temple is semi-circular, with a curved colonnade enclosing the apse area, adding architectural interest and symmetry. The roof is flat and open in the center, bordered by a raised parapet, and the overall exterior is finished in a light stone or concrete material. Decorative elements are minimal, focusing on the repetition of columns and geometric forms rather than intricate carvings or reliefs. The structure sits on a raised platform, further emphasizing its monumental and ceremonial character.
description: A rectangular, columned temple with a grand staircase, triangular pediment, and a semi-circular rear colonnade, finished in light stone. The design is minimalist, with strong geometric lines and a flat, open roof.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide staircase, columned portico, and triangular pediment, emphasizing the main entrance.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the long row of columns and the rectangular layout, with the semi-circular rear visible at the far end.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the curved colonnade enclosing the apse, with the open roof and raised parapet visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the colonnade and the semi-circular rear, with the entrance less prominent.
attribute_ornamentation: minimalist, geometric repetition of columns, no visible intricate carvings or reliefs
attribute_entrance_design: Wide staircase leading to a columned portico with a triangular pediment, framed by robust columns and a simple entablature, no visible ornate detailing
